page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-32-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor operating at 2.3 GHz. The instance features 32 virtual CPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 248.0 GB of RAM, providing a high resource density of 7.75 GB of memory per core. The processor includes a large 504 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. Local storage is bundled, consisting of 1745 GB of NVMe SSD capacity spread across 5 local drives.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with the single-core Geekbench score and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression rates placing in the top 10% tier. Multi-core performance is also robust, with the Passmark CPU score and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression ranking in the top 25%. For specialized workloads, the server demonstrates strong capabilities in Redis SET operations, handling 62.26 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput at 59.7 GB/sec. However, memory bandwidth benchmarks for both read and write operations on large blocks remain in the average tier, as does multi-core stress-ng CPU performance.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highmem-32-lssd offers high resource density by combining substantial memory capacity with fast local NVMe SSD storage on a dedicated hardware class. This configuration avoids the overhead of external network storage, making it highly efficient for data-intensive applications. The server is well-suited for high-throughput database operations, in-memory caching systems like Redis, text and image processing pipelines, and local LLM inference tasks using small to medium models, where its AMX instruction support can be leveraged.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-32-lssd is an x86_64 server configured with 32 vCPUs (16 physical cores) powered by a dedicated 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor. It features 248.0 GB of RAM, a 504 MB L3 cache, and 1745 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). Benchmarks show top-tier single-core performance, particularly in text and image processing, and strong multi-core capabilities in Redis operations and static web serving. However, large-block memory bandwidth and multi-core stress-ng performance fall into the average tier. Qualitatively, the server provides high resource density by bundling substantial memory with fast local storage. It is highly suited for memory-intensive databases, Redis caching, text processing, and small-to-medium LLM inference workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-32-lssd is an x86_64 server with 32 vCPUs, 248 GB RAM, and 1745 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-32-lssd features 32 vCPUs, 248 GB RAM, and 1745 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-memory x86_64 server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, advanced matrix extensions, and fast local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with 32 vCPUs and 248 GB RAM.
Bundled 1745 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage.
Top-tier single-core performance in text and image processing benchmarks.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations.
Well-suited for memory-intensive databases, Redis caching, and LLM inference.
